novel_features.name: Explainable facility resolution
novel_features.description: Rank bounded facility matches while preserving FRS identifiers, addresses, program status fields, and query provenance.

novel_features.name: Facility dossier change set
novel_features.description: Compare a live detailed facility report with the prior local snapshot and list changed ECHO sections without assigning a score.

novel_features.name: Portfolio compliance watch
novel_features.description: Fetch a bounded CSV portfolio of facility IDs and atomically report changed detailed-report sections since the prior run.

novel_features.name: Explained nearby scan
novel_features.description: List facilities within an EPA-supported radius and expose the compliance, inspection, action, and penalty fields behind each concern.

novel_features.name: Effluent exceedance trend
novel_features.description: Return ECHO's reported Clean Water Act effluent compliance and exceedance records for one stable facility identifier.

novel_features.name: Enforcement chain timeline
novel_features.description: Collect dated inspection, violation, formal-action, and enforcement sections from a detailed facility report without causal inference.

narrative.value_prop: Resolve EPA-regulated facilities, inspect transparent compliance evidence, and retain change history without creating opaque risk scores.

quickstart.comment: Resolve a name to source-preserving FRS matches.
Create or compare a detailed facility snapshot.

narrative.when_to_use: Use for query-only facility compliance research and reproducible ECHO snapshot changes.
anti_triggers: Do not use a result as a composite environmental risk score.
Do not assume similarly named facilities are the same entity.
Use EPA bulk downloads for large-volume analysis.

recipes.title: Explain nearby facilities
recipes.explanation: Return bounded nearby facilities with the ECHO fields driving each concern flag.

recipes.title: Order enforcement evidence
recipes.explanation: Keep reported actions and penalties attached to their source sections.
